title: transaction â€” PHP attribute contracts
description: Three class-level attributes. `#[AsTransactionListener]` subscribes a service to transaction-lane events (transaction lifecycle + ledger imbalance + reconciliation). `#[AsLedgerAccount]` marks a class as a chart-of-accounts registrant â€” enables data-driven per-tenant enable/disable of accounts. `#[AsTransactionKind]` marks a class as a transaction-kind handler â€” defines the canonical debit/credit pair for a given kind.

attributes.name: AsTransactionListener
attributes.description: Marks a service as a subscriber to transaction-lane events (TransactionCreated / Posted / Reversed / AmendmentRequested, LedgerEntryRecorded, LedgerImbalanceDetected, AccountBalanceThresholdCrossed, TenantOpeningBalanceRecorded, TransactionCurrencyConverted, LedgerReconciliationCompleted). Discovery loader picks up each class at boot + wires the declared events. Consumers of LedgerImbalanceDetected should override queue to `finance-critical` since imbalances are P0 signals.

params.name: events
params.description: Array of event short names (`TransactionPosted`, `LedgerImbalanceDetected`, `TransactionReversed`, â€¦). Empty = subscribe to every `Stackra\Finance\Transaction\Events\*` event. Names resolve against events.json at boot; typos abort boot with a discovery error.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue override. Falls back to the event's default queue (see events.json). LedgerImbalanceDetected + TransactionAmendmentRequested consumers should specify 'finance-critical'.

params.name: priority
params.description: Handler ordering within a single event. Lower runs first. The HaltNewTransactionsOnImbalance listener runs at priority 10 so it sets the halt flag before any downstream listener processes the imbalance signal.

attributes.name: AsLedgerAccount
attributes.description: Marks a class as a chart-of-accounts registrant. The ChartOfAccountsRegistry discovers these at boot + registers the account in the module's account catalog. Every #[AsLedgerAccount] class MUST implement the ILedgerAccount contract (slug(), display_name(), regulator_category(), default_currency()).

params.name: slug
params.description: Account slug matching the LedgerAccount enum (revenue / accounts_receivable / cash / refunds_owed / refunds_expense / tax_payable / gateway_fees / bank_deposits / write_offs / coupons_expense / chargebacks_expense / accounts_payable / prepayments / deferred_revenue). One class per slug.

params.name: regulator_category
params.description: Enum: asset / liability / equity / revenue / expense. Regulator-visible classification for GL exports. Every GL report groups accounts by regulator_category. Example: cash + accounts_receivable + bank_deposits + prepayments = asset; accounts_payable + refunds_owed + tax_payable + deferred_revenue = liability; revenue = revenue; refunds_expense + gateway_fees + coupons_expense + chargebacks_expense + write_offs = expense.

params.name: default_currency
params.description: Optional default currency for the account. When null, the tenant's base_currency applies. Multi-currency ledgers may pin certain accounts to specific currencies (e.g. tax_payable pinned to the tenant's jurisdiction currency).

params.name: enabled_by_default
params.description: true = the account is enabled for every newly-provisioned tenant. false = the account is opt-in (rare â€” reserved for specialty accounts that only apply to certain tenant types).

attributes.name: AsTransactionKind
attributes.description: Marks a class as a transaction-kind handler. Defines the canonical debit/credit pair for a given TransactionKind â€” the TransactionRecorder consults these classes when building ledger entries. Every #[AsTransactionKind] class MUST implement the ITransactionKindHandler contract (buildLedgerEntries(TransactionData): TransactionLedgerEntryData[]).

params.name: kind
params.description: TransactionKind enum value (invoice_payment / refund_issued / chargeback_debit / coupon_credit / tax_remittance / fee_charge / adjustment / write_off / bank_deposit / bank_withdrawal / currency_conversion / opening_balance). One class per kind.

params.name: canonical_entries
params.description: Documented expected [debit_account, credit_account] pairs for this kind. Read at boot into data/sample-double-entry-patterns.json. Example: invoice_payment â†’ [{debit: 'cash', credit: 'accounts_receivable'}]. refund_issued â†’ [{debit: 'refunds_owed', credit: 'cash'}, {debit: 'refunds_expense', credit: 'revenue'}] (two ledger transaction pairs â€” recorded as two separate Transaction rows sharing source_transaction_id).
